commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `talk.config(includeSecrets:true)` so scoped clients get runtime-resolved Talk provider config in `talk.resolved.config`, with gateway and iOS parser regression coverage.

PR surface: Source -4, Tests +66, Other +39. Total +101 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main returns the source include-secrets payload before runtime resolution, while the iOS parser only treats a string `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` as configured. I did not run a live gateway/native-client reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining work is maintainer handling of the protected label, credential-boundary confirmation, real behavior proof, and CI triage rather than a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: Fix Talk SecretRef config payloads for native clients
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `talk.config(includeSecrets:true)` so scoped clients receive the runtime-resolved Talk provider config instead of the source SecretRef payload.
- Keep read-scope behavior safe: source SecretRef shape is still preserved for UI context and redacted when `includeSecrets` is not allowed.
- Add gateway and iOS parser regression coverage for native ElevenLabs Talk config.

## Problem

Native Talk docs describe `talk.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey` as the credential used by configured Talk playback, including fallback from `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` (`docs/nodes/talk.md:64`, `docs/nodes/talk.md:111`). The secrets runtime already resolves `talk.providers.*.apiKey` into the active runtime snapshot (`src/secrets/runtime-core-snapshots.test.ts:159`).

The broken part was the `talk.config` response path. When a first-party client requested `includeSecrets:true`, `src/gateway/server-methods/talk.ts:451` now needs to return the provider-resolved config to scoped clients. Before this patch, the handler returned the raw payload early, so native clients could receive the source SecretRef object instead of a usable string in `talk.resolved.config.apiKey`.

That matters for iOS because the app first requests `talk.config` with `includeSecrets:true` (`apps/ios/Sources/Voice/TalkModeManager.swift:2440`) and the parser only treats `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` as configured when it is a string (`apps/ios/Sources/Voice/TalkModeGatewayConfig.swift:225`). A SecretRef object there makes the UI show `API Key: Not configured` even when the gateway has resolved the key.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Native Talk clients with `operator.talk.secrets` now receive a usable string in `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` when the source config uses a SecretRef.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS 26.5, Node 22 via Nix, and iPhone 17 Pro iOS Simulator 26.5.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `nix shell --impure --accept-flake-config nixpkgs#nodejs_22 nixpkgs#pnpm -c node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/server.talk-config.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: Gateway focused proof passed with `Test Files 4 passed (4)` and `Tests 76 passed (76)`.

Observed result after fix: The gateway tests prove read-scope callers still see redacted SecretRef context, while scoped include-secrets callers get source `providers.acme.apiKey` as the SecretRef and `resolved.config.apiKey` as the resolved string.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `xcodebuildmcp simulator test --project-path apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj --scheme OpenClawLogicTests --simulator-id 36015582-9277-42FE-BBDE-DFF4AD63437E`

Evidence after fix: iOS logic tests passed with `Total: 7`, `Passed: 7`, `Failed: 0`.

Observed result after fix: The iOS parser test proves native ElevenLabs Talk reads the string API key from `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` while leaving the source provider SecretRef payload in `talk.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey`.

What was not tested: No live ElevenLabs API key or billing account was used, and no physical iOS app session against a deployed gateway was run in this PR.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `nix shell --impure --accept-flake-config nixpkgs#nodejs_22 nixpkgs#pnpm -c pnpm docs:list`
- `nix shell --impure --accept-flake-config nixpkgs#nodejs_22 nixpkgs#pnpm -c node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/server.talk-config.test.ts`
- `xcodebuildmcp project-discovery list-schemes --project-path apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj`
- `xcodebuildmcp simulator list`
- `xcodebuildmcp simulator test --project-path apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj --scheme OpenClawLogicTests --simulator-id 36015582-9277-42FE-BBDE-DFF4AD63437E`
